mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating household income and consumer spending power, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of instances when unemployment exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies revenue growth expectations and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient real economic activity that outpaces rate hike headwinds, triggering short-term positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of tightening cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth exceeding 0.3 percent month-over-month forces central banks to accelerate tightening, which raises borrowing costs and suppresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 20-day returns in more than 70 percent of months where CPI inflation surpasses the 0.3 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports wage-driven demand, which systematically lifts corporate earnings and generates positive quarterly stock returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across all rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent while unemployment rises indicates a demand collapse that erodes profit forecasts and triggers negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of weak GDP and rising unemployment fails to predict negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices breaking above 110 signal robust manufacturing capacity utilization that outpaces inflationary concerns, generating immediate positive equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain negative or flat in more than 55 percent of months where the industrial production index exceeds 110.
